programme.title: Live PD: Greatest Shifts

programme.title: Live PD: Greatest Shifts

programme.title: Long Lost Family

programme.title: Poh's Kitchen

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Anh's Brush With Fame
programme.desc: Jamie Durie is one of our most dynamic TV personalities. He's led an interesting life that has had its challenges. Can Anh capture his charisma and passion?

programme.title: Restoration Australia

programme.desc: Detective Murdoch's investigation into the disappearance of a quiet librarian leads to a reunion with former love Anna Fulford and the discovery of a seamy underworld.

programme.title: Long Lost Family

programme.desc: Geoffrey Wansell explores the case of George Shotton, who murdered his wife near Swansea in 1919. This episode features expert interviews.

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's Killer Evidence
programme.desc: A homeless man finds human limbs wrapped in black bin bags. It's the start of a trail of forensic clues that will eventually lead police to one of London's most shocking serial killers.

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's UK Killer Evidence
programme.desc: Pennie Davis from Hampshire was killed in 2014, after a revived feud led Ben Carr to hire a hitman.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Spain holds a special place in Mark and Gill's hearts and they dream of owning a home on the magical Mar Menor. Can Laura Hamilton find their perfect place for £60k.

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Stained-glass artist Kate welcomes us aboard her floating workshop. Rob and Ade set off on an adventure. Stuart heads home from Hemel Hempstead.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Families buy and renovate chateaux in France. After selling Château de Saugé, Donna and Paul start a new adventure at a dilapidated water mill in Normandy.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners hunt for the perfect party pad for party animals Kevin and Sarah. In Alicante, the Rodriguezes help Rosie and Barry find a bolthole.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programme.title: Invasion of the Parakeets
programme.desc: Ring-necked parakeets are some of Britain's most divisive birds. Are they beautiful, charismatic newcomers, or an ecological menace to crops and native wildlife? Chris Packham investigates.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Sunny is rushed to Queen's with multiple stab wounds. Tyler's hurt his foot playing football. And doctors are concerned about an 87-year-old woman who's coughing up blood.

programme.title: Dolly: The World's Most Famous Sheep
programme.desc: In 1996, a team of Scottish scientists made Dolly, the first mammal cloned from an adult cell, and changed history. From global fame to moral uproar, this is Dolly's story.

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man is experiencing life-threatening diabetic hypoglycaemia after falling down a flight of stairs. And a woman may have injured her side while sleepwalking.

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: Castle and Beckett team up with a handsome Scotland Yard detective to solve the murder of a British model.

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: Castle decides to shadow another detective - a boorish maverick from the city's gang unit whose last three partners were killed in the line of duty.

programme.title: Love Nature

programme.title: Little Disasters
